data.title: Capri's credit rating downgraded to junk by S&P
data.description:
Citing expectations for continued negative sales trends, missteps in repositioning its Versace and Michael Kors labels, and high leverage, S&P Global downgraded Capri’s (NYSE:CPRI) credit rating to BB from BBB- and lowered its outlook to Negative from Negative Watch.

“The negative outlook reflects risks in the company’s plans to turn around the business in the coming year in a cost-effective manner. We remain cautious about recent strategic missteps, the weak macroeconomic environment, and go-forward strategies to return to brand strength and improve profitability,” the ratings agency said regarding its decision.

S&P also notes that while Capri’s (NYSE:CPRI) acquisition of Jimmy Choo and Versace increased its presence in Europe and Asia, weak global demand for luxury goods has weighed on the company’s overall performance.

“We believe the company has lost market share to other competitors, not all of which are experiencing similar declines,” S&P said, adding that given Capri’s (NYSE:CPRI) underperformance in the business across all regions and brands coupled with a lack of strategic initiative as the company’s performance deteriorated amid the failed Tapestry (TPR) deal, S&P revised its management and governance score to moderately negative from positive.

In addition, any efforts to turn the business around appear to exceed Capri’s (CPRI) current resources, including cash on hand and availability in its revolver facility.

S&P would reconsider its negative rating if the company were successful in executing its strategy to improve sales and profitability which would bring its leverage-to-EBTIDA ratio “comfortably” below 4x on a sustained basis.

Capri (CPRI) shares are down by more than 3%.

data.title: Gannett targets 7%-10% digital revenue growth in 2025 with focus on audience monetization
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Gannett (NYSE:GCI) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Mike Reed highlighted that 2024 marked significant progress for Gannett, achieving milestones such as adjusted EBITDA growth, free cash flow improvements, and strengthened digital revenue trends. Digital revenues increased to $1.1 billion, accounting for 44% of total revenue, with expectations for this to grow to 50% in 2025.
  • Reed emphasized the company’s focus on digital transformation, stating, "In 2025, our expectation is that total digital revenues will grow between 7% and 10% on a same-store basis, surpassing secular declines in print revenue." He also noted improvements in audience engagement, with 200 million average monthly unique visitors and double-digit page view growth.
  • Kristin Roberts, Chief Content Officer, announced initiatives to expand sports content and video journalism, aiming to deepen audience engagement. She stated that USA TODAY NETWORK generated over 390 million page views during the football season and plans to grow its coverage in women’s sports and video content in 2025.
  • CFO Doug Horne reported Q4 total operating revenues of $621.3 million and adjusted EBITDA of $78.2 million. He noted a 17% increase in digital-only subscription revenue and a 13% rise in average revenue per user.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its 2025 guidance for adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow growth, supported by digital revenue expansion. Total digital revenues are projected to grow between 7% and 10% on a same-store basis, with digital revenues expected to contribute 50% of total revenue.
  • Reed stated that the company is "targeting the inflection point to positive revenue growth during the year," driven by improvements in digital advertising, subscription growth, and audience monetization.
  • Horne provided guidance for Q1 2025, expecting same-store revenue trends to align with Q4 2024 before sequential improvements begin in Q2, with positive overall revenue growth anticipated by year-end.

Financial Results

  • For Q4 2024, Gannett reported adjusted EBITDA of $78.2 million, up 5.5% year-over-year, with adjusted EBITDA margin improving to 12.6%.
  • Digital-only subscription revenue increased 17% year-over-year, supported by double-digit growth in average revenue per user. Total digital revenues reached $280.4 million, representing 45.1% of total revenues.
  • Operating expenses decreased by approximately 6% in Q4, attributed to strategic cost controls and operational efficiency measures. Free cash flow for the full year totaled $58.4 million, up 3.5% year-over-year.
  • The company’s net income for Q4 was $64.3 million, with adjusted net income of $38.3 million, reflecting significant year-over-year improvements.

Q&A

  • Giuliano Bologna, Compass Point: Questioned the contribution of Digital Marketing Solutions (DMS) to the projected 7%-10% digital revenue growth for 2025. Mike Reed explained that digital advertising would contribute consistently throughout the year, while DMS growth would accelerate in the second half of 2025.
  • Bologna also inquired about asset sales and debt paydowns. Reed confirmed that $50-$60 million in proceeds from Q1 asset sales would go toward debt reduction, with further sales expected to generate $60-$70 million in 2025.
  • Matthew Condon, JMP Securities: Asked about strategies for digital subscriber growth. Reed outlined plans to personalize user experiences and increase local content focus, leveraging data to optimize user engagement and subscriptions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of digital transformation and the impact of asset sales on revenue. The tone was neutral to slightly positive, reflecting confidence in the 2025 guidance but cautious optimism regarding execution.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, with Reed stating, "We are extremely confident in the large opportunities related to our DMS business and the inflection point to positive revenue growth in 2025." In the Q&A, Reed remained consistent in emphasizing the company’s digital transformation strategy and debt reduction plans.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment from both analysts and management showed increased confidence due to improved financial results and clear progress in digital initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management’s guidance language shifted from stabilization in Q3 to growth expectations in Q4, with a stronger emphasis on achieving positive revenue growth in 2025.
  • Key metrics such as adjusted EBITDA and digital-only subscription revenue showed consistent improvement from Q3 to Q4.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from operational stabilization in Q3 to questions about long-term growth drivers, particularly in digital advertising and DMS.
  • Management’s confidence in digital initiatives, including audience monetization and subscription growth, was more evident in Q4 compared to the cautious optimism in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged challenges in the DMS segment, including macroeconomic headwinds and customer churn, but outlined strategies to improve retention and expand product offerings.
  • Reed noted risks related to Google’s algorithm changes, which impacted partnership revenues, emphasizing the need for flexibility in an evolving search environment.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the impact of asset sales on revenue and the timeline for achieving positive revenue growth.

Final Takeaway

Gannett’s Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted the company’s progress in digital transformation, with strong growth in digital-only subscriptions and adjusted EBITDA. Management is confident in achieving 7%-10% digital revenue growth in 2025, driven by audience monetization and expansion in digital advertising and DMS. Asset sales are expected to support continued debt reduction, while strategic investments in content and technology aim to sustain long-term growth. Analysts remain cautiously optimistic, focusing on execution risks and revenue inflection timelines.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Pan American Silver outlines 2025 production guidance with silver at 20-21M oz and gold at 735-800K oz
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pan American Silver Corp. (NYSE:PAAS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Michael Steinmann, President and CEO, highlighted record revenue of $815.1M in Q4 and $2.8B for 2024, driven by production from acquired Yamana mines and favorable metal prices. Silver output totaled 21.1M ounces, while gold production hit a company record of 892,000 ounces.
  • Cash flow from operations reached $274.1M in Q4 and $724.1M for the full year. Free cash flow was $196.2M in Q4 and $445.1M for 2024. The company is in a net cash position, with $887M in cash and short-term investments against $800M in debt.
  • Adjusted earnings for Q4 were $126.9M, or $0.35 per share, while full-year adjusted earnings stood at $286.7M, or $0.79 per share. The company declared a $0.10 per share dividend for Q4 and repurchased $20M worth of shares in Q1 2025.
  • Strategic projects completed in 2024 include new ventilation infrastructure at La Colorada, a filtration plant at Huaron, and a paste backfill plant at Timmins, aimed at enhancing operational efficiency and lowering costs.
  • CFO stated that "the company’s liquidity position, combined with its operational success, positions Pan American to pursue growth initiatives while maintaining shareholder returns."

Outlook

  • For 2025, Pan American expects silver production between 20M and 21M ounces and gold production between 735,000 and 800,000 ounces. Production is anticipated to be weighted toward the second half of the year.
  • All-in sustaining costs for the silver segment are forecasted at $16.25–$18.25 per ounce, while gold segment costs are estimated at $1,525–$1,625 per ounce.
  • Sustaining capital is projected at $270M–$285M, with project capital between $90M and $100M, focusing on La Colorada, Timmins, Jacobina, and Huaron.
  • Exploration spending in 2025 is estimated at $80M, primarily for reserve replacement.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net earnings were $107.8M, or $0.30 per share, influenced by a gain from the La Arena sale and adjustments at Dolores related to its transition to residual leaching. Adjusted earnings excluded these one-time items.
  • 2024 silver segment all-in sustaining costs were $18.98 per ounce, slightly above guidance, while gold segment costs were $1,501 per ounce, aligning with expectations.
  • Record quarterly silver and gold production was supported by operational improvements at La Colorada and Yamana acquisitions.

Q&A

  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Asked about La Colorada’s 2025 guidance of 5.5–5.8M ounces of silver. Steve Busby, COO, explained that the forecast balances short-term constraints and long-term value creation, including waste movement and developing new reserves in the eastern zone.
  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Requested details on Jacobina’s optimization studies. Busby shared that infrastructure upgrades, including screen replacements and tank upgrades, will stabilize throughput, with results expected mid-2025.
  • Don DeMarco, National Bank Financial: Inquired about El Peñon’s stable production and costs. Busby confirmed operational stability and a focus on extending mine life through exploration.
  • Adrian Day, Adrian Day Asset Management: Asked about the timeline for Escobal’s restart. Steinmann estimated production could resume within one to two quarters after receiving government approval, with full ramp-up taking about a year.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a positive tone, focusing on stabilization and strong free cash flow generation. Questions centered on production guidance, cost management, and strategic developments.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing record performance and financial strength. During Q&A, responses were detailed, reflecting transparency and optimism in long-term initiatives.
  • Compared to Q3, both analysts and management displayed increased confidence, driven by operational improvements and a robust financial position.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue increased significantly compared to Q3, supported by higher production and robust metal prices. Free cash flow and adjusted earnings also showed substantial quarter-over-quarter growth.
  • The tone of management in Q4 was more optimistic, reflecting confidence in operational stability and financial strength. Analysts shifted focus from short-term challenges to a broader outlook on 2025 guidance.
  • Strategic priorities evolved, with greater emphasis on long-term growth projects such as La Colorada Skarn, Jacobina optimization, and exploration spending.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges at Dolores include the transition to residual leaching and adjustments to decommissioning liabilities. Management has accounted for these in its guidance.
  • Delayed consultation processes at Escobal could impact timelines for its restart. Management continues to engage with stakeholders to advance the process.
  • High sustaining capital requirements and cost inflation remain areas to monitor, although management highlighted mitigation strategies through operational efficiencies.

Final Takeaway

Pan American Silver reported record financial and operational performance in Q4 2024, driven by strong production and integration of Yamana mines. The company enters 2025 with a robust financial position, projecting steady silver and gold production while focusing on strategic growth initiatives and cost management. Continued progress at key assets like La Colorada and Jacobina, alongside exploration investments, positions the company for long-term value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Walgreens Boots drops on report Sycamore bidding for Family Dollar chain
data.description:
Walgreen Boots (NASDAQ:WBA) fell quickly fell 4.8% on a report that Sycamore Partners is competing to buy the Family Dollar chain of stores from Dollar Tree (DLTR).

The Reuters story comes as Sycamore Partners reportedly has been interested in a takeover of Walgreens Boots (NASDAQ:WBA). On Monday CNBC's David Faber said that there are still signs of life with Sycamore's takeover talks with the pharmacy chain, sending Walgreens shares soaring 14%.

Reuters on Thursday reported that Sycamore is competing with Apollo Global to buy the Family Dollar chain from Dollar Tree (DLTR).

The WSJ first reported in December that Sycamore was in talks to take Walgreens private.

data.title: HNI targets double-digit EPS growth in 2025 amid operational improvements and expanded investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: HNI Corporation (NYSE:HNI) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Jeff Lorenger noted that non-GAAP EPS for 2024 reached $3.06, a 15% year-over-year increase, marking the third consecutive year of double-digit growth. Workplace Furnishings achieved a 44% increase in non-GAAP operating profit with a 9.5% margin, the highest since 2007, while Residential Building Products maintained strong margins at 17.5% despite housing market challenges.
  • The company anticipates a fourth consecutive year of double-digit EPS growth in 2025, driven by margin expansion efforts and revenue growth investments in both Workplace Furnishings and Residential Building Products.
  • CFO Vincent Paul Berger emphasized targeted investments in sales capabilities, product development, and digital connectivity to drive future growth. He also highlighted effective cost management strategies, including actions to lower Residential Building Products’ cost structure by $5 million, with most benefits expected in 2025.

Outlook

  • First-quarter 2025 non-GAAP EPS is expected to decline slightly due to temporary tariff-related costs but is projected to recover over subsequent quarters. Management estimates tariff-related price cost drag to be $3 million to $4 million in Q1, with full-year impacts mitigated through surcharges, supplier concessions, and productivity initiatives.
  • Full-year 2025 revenue is forecasted to grow at a low to mid-single-digit rate across both segments, with Workplace Furnishings expected to return to revenue growth in Q2. Residential Building Products growth will be driven by remodel retrofit and new construction markets, with acceleration anticipated in the second half of the year.
  • Synergies from the Kimball International acquisition and the Mexico facility are expected to contribute $0.70 to $0.80 in EPS growth through 2026, representing approximately 25% growth on top of 2024 earnings.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 non-GAAP EPS was $0.87 compared to analysts' estimate of $0.74. Revenue for the quarter was $657 million vs. analysts' estimate of $657.03 million. This performance exceeded expectations due to productivity improvements and limited decremental margins.
  • Workplace Furnishings experienced a 2% increase in segment orders in Q4, driven by improved contract customer activity, while SMB transactional business remained soft. Residential Building Products revenue declined 5% year-over-year, reflecting continued housing market weakness.
  • The company generated over $225 million in operating cash flow in 2024 and returned $129 million to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks. Year-end gross debt leverage stood at 1.1x, unchanged from Q3.

Q&A

  • Reuben Garner, Benchmark: Asked about investment priorities in Workplace Furnishings. CEO Jeff Lorenger explained that investments are being made in sales capabilities, customer experience, product development, and digital connectivity to capitalize on growth opportunities in verticals like healthcare, education, and hospitality.
  • Greg Burns, Sidoti: Inquired about SMB demand trends. CEO Lorenger noted that SMB activity remains flat due to economic uncertainty but expressed confidence in a potential rebound in the second half of 2025.
  • Steven Ramsey, Thompson Research Group: Asked about extended sales cycles in Workplace Furnishings. CEO Lorenger attributed the lengthened timeline to complex real estate decisions, inflation-driven budget adjustments, and cautious customer behavior, which are factored into 2025 guidance.
  • Brian Gordon, Water Tower Research: Queried return-to-office momentum. CEO Lorenger observed increased activity and acknowledged that return-to-office transitions continue to generate furniture demand across different sectors.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were cautiously optimistic, focusing on near-term challenges like SMB softness and tariff impacts while recognizing the company's long-term growth potential through operational improvements and investments.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing their ability to manage cost pressures and deliver sustained earnings growth. The tone in prepared remarks was more assertive compared to the previous quarter, highlighting growth opportunities despite macroeconomic uncertainties.
  • During the Q&A, management demonstrated a constructive approach, though slightly defensive when addressing SMB demand and tariff-related concerns.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from a focus on managing near-term demand volatility in Q3 to emphasizing revenue growth investments and operational improvements in Q4.
  • Workplace Furnishings backlog and order funnel metrics improved quarter-over-quarter, supporting expectations for revenue growth in 2025.
  • Analysts' concerns remained consistent, focusing on SMB demand softness and extended sales cycles, while management conveyed increased confidence in their growth initiatives and cost management strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted demand volatility in SMB and housing markets as ongoing risks. Tariff uncertainties and potential cost pressures from new tariffs remain a concern.
  • Mitigation strategies include implementing surcharges, enhancing supplier negotiations, and maintaining productivity initiatives to offset tariff impacts.
  • Analysts highlighted potential risks related to prolonged SMB softness and execution challenges in achieving growth targets amid macroeconomic headwinds.

Final Takeaway

HNI Corporation ended 2024 on a strong note, achieving record non-GAAP EPS and operational margin improvements despite challenging market conditions. The company’s outlook for 2025 emphasizes investments in growth and operational synergies, supported by strong backlog metrics and internal cost control measures. While near-term challenges such as SMB softness and tariff impacts persist, management remains confident in achieving another year of double-digit EPS growth, driven by strategic initiatives and favorable long-term market fundamentals.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tenaris outlines 10% volume rebound for Q1 2025 with stable margins amid tariff uncertainties
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Tenaris S.A. (NYSE:TS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Chairman and CEO Paolo Rocca highlighted strong 2024 financial performance, reporting an EBITDA of $3.1 billion and net income of $2.1 billion on net sales of $12.5 billion. He noted increased shareholder returns, including a proposed 38% annual dividend increase.
  • Rocca emphasized new strategic deepwater and pipeline projects, including contracts with Shell and BP for U.S. deepwater operations, and a major pipeline project in Argentina to connect Vaca Muerta to a deepwater port.
  • COO Gabriel Podskubka detailed progress in the Middle East, including a $250 million award for a major CCS pipeline in Saudi Arabia and an extension of a long-term agreement with ADNOC in Abu Dhabi.
  • CFO Alicia Mondolo announced a net cash position of $3.6 billion at the end of the quarter, while discussing the impact of an interim dividend payment and $454 million spent on share buybacks.

Outlook

  • Management expects Q1 2025 volumes and revenues to rebound approximately 10% compared to Q4 2024, with EBITDA margins remaining stable.
  • Rocca noted that tariffs under Section 232 could drive U.S. OCTG pricing upwards, supported by the company’s strong domestic manufacturing presence.
  • Activity in Argentina is projected to increase, with an expected rise in active drilling rigs from 31 to over 42 by year-end 2025. Investments in fracking capacity are being expanded to meet rising demand.
  • Rocca stated, "We expect to see a slight increase in margins during the second quarter of 2025, contingent on U.S. tariff outcomes."

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 net sales reached $2.8 billion, down 2% sequentially, influenced by lower volumes and average selling prices, partially offset by a favorable product mix.
  • EBITDA increased 6% sequentially to $726 million, benefiting from a one-off provision reversal. Excluding this, EBITDA was $659 million, reflecting a margin of 23%.
  • Cash flow from operations was $492 million, while significant outflows included $454 million in share buybacks and $182 million in capital expenditures.
  • The Board proposed an annual dividend of $0.83 per share, including a $0.27 interim dividend already paid.

Q&A

  • Arun Jayaram, JPMorgan Securities LLC: Asked about the potential impact of 25% tariffs on imported steel tubulars in the U.S. Paolo Rocca responded, "We expect U.S. OCTG pricing to gradually increase, as imports play a significant role in the market. Tenaris is well-positioned to manage this due to our extensive domestic manufacturing capabilities."
  • Alessandro Pozzi, Mediobanca: Inquired about Q1 2025 sales and margins. Rocca clarified that Q4 2024 volumes would decline by single digits, while Q1 2025 sales and volumes are projected to increase by 10%.
  • Derek Podhaizer, Piper Sandler: Sought clarity on U.S. supply-demand dynamics. Luca Zanotti, President of U.S. Operations, noted a significant reduction in imports and an inventory normalization to approximately six months.
  • Kevin Roger, Kepler Cheuvreux: Queried the impact of tariffs on production costs. Rocca explained that Tenaris sources steel both domestically and internationally, with flexibility to adjust supply chains based on tariff outcomes.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a mix of skepticism and optimism. Concerns centered on tariff uncertainties and sales declines in Q4 2024, while confidence was noted regarding Q1 2025 guidance and strategic positioning in the U.S.
  • Management maintained a confident yet cautious tone, emphasizing readiness to adapt to potential tariff changes and highlighting strategic achievements. Rocca stated, "We are confident that Tenaris is well-positioned to navigate uncertainties ahead."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated a more assured tone regarding U.S. pricing trends and Argentina’s energy sector growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 net sales of $2.8 billion were slightly lower than Q3’s $2.9 billion, reflecting continued pricing pressures and volume declines.
  • EBITDA improved sequentially in Q4 2024 due to a one-off provision reversal, whereas Q3 results were hindered by extraordinary litigation provisions.
  • Management tone in Q4 reflected greater confidence in U.S. OCTG market recovery, bolstered by tariff-driven pricing expectations and reduced imports.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused heavily on tariffs and U.S. supply-demand dynamics, shifting from Q3 concerns over broader geopolitical uncertainties.

Risks and Concerns

  • Geopolitical risks, including potential changes to U.S. tariffs and Section 232 quotas, were highlighted as significant uncertainties.
  • Rocca acknowledged challenges in Mexico, citing Pemex’s financial difficulties and reduced investment as key factors limiting activity.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential delays in Argentina’s pipeline projects and Pemex receivables, which stood at approximately $140 million.

Final Takeaway

Tenaris highlighted a robust outlook for Q1 2025, projecting a 10% increase in volumes and revenues while maintaining stable EBITDA margins. Strategic wins in deepwater projects and pipeline contracts underline its industry leadership, while tariff uncertainties and challenges in Mexico and Argentina remain key risks to monitor.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Hockey drama: Sportsbooks get a boost by heavy interest in the U.S. playing Canada
data.description:
The hockey world has been energized by a tantalizing championship game set for Thursday night in the 4 Nations Face-Off. The marquee match-up of the U.S. playing against Canada continues a long tradition of high-profile championships matchups between the two nations dating back to Canada Cup and Olympic finals. An added twist this year is the Trump Administration's threat of tariffs against Canada that has stirred up patriotic stances on both sides.

For investors, the mid-season tournament has also generated a significant level of sports betting interest. BetMGM (NYSE:MGM) (OTCPK:GMVHF) reported that 4 of the 6 most bet hockey games of the season have occurred over the last week from the 4 Nations tournament. The championship game on Thursday is already the most bet hockey game of the season by number of tickets.

Notably, the preliminary U.S.-Canada game from last Saturday was the most bet hockey game of the season. "We expect the USA-Canada final to be the most bet hockey game of the year. Canada winning would be a good outcome for the sportsbook," stated BetMGM Senior Trading Manager Christian Cipollini.

That all adds up to the potential for a Q1 earnings boost for sports betting operators such as DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG), BetMGM, FanDuel owner Flutter Entertainment (FLUT), and Caesars Entertainment (CZR) from the unexpected surge in interest in hockey betting during a typically quiet period on the betting calendar.

data.title: Apollo, Sycamore competing to buy Family Dollar chain from Dollar Tree - Reuters
data.description:
Private equity firms Apollo Global (APO) and Sycamore Partners are competing to purchase the Family Dollar chain from Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR). Dollar Tree rose 3.5%.

Investment firm Brigade Capital Management has also shown interest in Family Dollar, according to a Reuters report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Family Dollar would be valued at several billion dollars in a deal, though any transaction isn't imminent, according to the report.

Dollar Tree didn't immediately respond to Reuters request for comment. Apollo, Brigade and Sycamore declined to comment.

Developing story ...

data.title: Ukraine President Zelenskyy says conversation with US envoy was 'good'
data.description:
Ukraine President Volodymr Zelenskyy said Thursday that he had a “good conversation” with the U.S. envoy for Russia and Ukraine about how to end the war in Ukraine following following Russia’s invasion nearly three years ago.

Zelenskyy earlier in the day met with Keith Kellogg in Kyiv. A news conference following their meeting was changed at the last moment to a photo opportunity with the two. They did not take questions.

The U.S. requested to cancel the news conference, according to media reports.

“I am grateful to the United States for all the assistance and bipartisan support for Ukraine and the Ukrainian people,” Zelenskyy said in a post on X.

“It’s important for us—and for the entire free world—that American strength is felt,” he added. ”We had a detailed conversation about the battlefield situation, how to return our prisoners of war, and effective security guarantees.”

Zelenskyy said the two discussed the “battlefield situation,” the return of prisoners of war, and “effective security guarantees.”

Thursday’s meeting came after the U.S. and Russia agreed earlier in the week to begin work on bringing the war to an end, without involving Ukraine.

President Donald Trump said Zelenskyy was unpopular in Ukraine and a dictator. Zelensky accused Trump of living in a “disinformation space” of Russia’s.

“Ukraine is ready for a strong, effective investment and security agreement with the President of the United States. We have proposed the fastest and most constructive way to achieve results. Our team is ready to work 24/7, “ Zelenskyy said Thursday. “Success unites us all. Strong Ukraine-U.S. relations benefit the entire world.”

data.title: Tuttle Capital to launch UFO-focused ETF targeting ‘alien technology’ inspired companies
data.description:
Tuttle Capital Management has filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to launch an ETF that is aimed at investing in businesses that may be connected to advanced "alien technologies" associated with unidentified flying objects ((UFOs)).

Per the SEC filing, the proposed fund is the Tuttle Capital UFO Disclosure AI Powered ETF which would trade under the ticker symbol UFOD.

Moreover, UFOD would focus on investing in companies with potential ties to advanced alien technology, spurred by government UFO disclosures. It invests in aerospace, defense, materials, energy, and semiconductor companies that could benefit from alien-inspired technologies.

The fund also targets firms specializing in UFO detection or sensor technology. Additionally, it employs short positions or swaps on companies at risk of obsolescence due to these advancements, like traditional propulsion and energy firms.

At this time no expense ratio or specific holdings have been listed but some potential names that may perhaps find themselves inside UFOD are aerospace and defense firms such as RTX Corporation (RTX), Lockheed Martin (LMT), and Boeing (BA). Or possibly semiconductor giants like Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC).

From a competitive standpoint, UFOD will likely face competition from two other ETFs: The Procure Space ETF (NASDAQ:UFO) and the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKX).
